chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No. D-1478 of 2023 (Syed Zain ul Abideen v. Fed. of Pakistan & others) Mr. Badaruddin Memon, Advocate along with petitioner. Mr. Mujeeb Rehman Shaikh, Advocate for respondents-SEPCO. Mr. Dareshani Ali Haider ‘Ada’, D.A.G. Date of Hearing & Order: 27-03-2024 O R D E R Petitioner, an electricity consumer under Meter No.SP-12756, is aggrieved by alleged incorrect bill issued to him by the SEPCO. 2. In response, respondent-SEPCO has filed comments. Their Advocate submits that already a summary has been moved for making correction in the electricity bills of the petitioner, on whose residence, in fact two meters have been installed and against one meter, Rs.10,15,877/- an outstanding amount, is pending. But in any case, correction in the bills and relaxation, as provided under the Rules and Policy of SEPCO, would be extended to the petitioner and he would be given relief in accordance with law. He undertakes that within two months either such exercise would be made. 3. Accordingly and in the light of above statement, petition is disposed of. However, after two months, compliance report be submitted for a perusal in chamber. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
